description:
=== Symptom ===
After a task with no code-changing work (gate tasks, doc-only work where main is already ahead, bugfixes that become no-ops after merging main in) goes through bl claim -> bl review -> bl close, two things are wrong:

1. The archived task's delivered_in field points at an unrelated commit on main — whatever happened to be at HEAD when bl review ran. Caller-confusing.
2. Future bl prime / bl sync calls warn:
     warning: state branch records close for bl-xxxx but no [bl-xxxx] tag reachable from main
   This is detect_half_push firing on what it thinks is a half-push (state branch pushed, main didn't). It's actually a no-op review — no commit was ever made on main because there was nothing to squash.

Reproduced in ~/dev/lernie: 11 archived gate tasks (bl-4ac8, bl-6796, bl-7569, bl-92f9, bl-3e02, bl-7ac1, bl-1b81, bl-1110, bl-b3f0, bl-a320, bl-517d) all hit this. E.g. bl-4ac8 ('Gate: docs up to date (schemas)') has delivered_in = e0609be, which is the [bl-5bbc] commit — its parent's delivery, not its own.

=== Root cause ===
src/review.rs review_worktree (line ~74) runs, for an empty-squash case:

  1. git_add_all + git_commit('wip: ...')  in worktree — src/git.rs:99 git_commit silently returns Ok when has_staged_changes is false and not mid-merge. OK.
  2. merge main into worktree — Already up to date. OK.
  3. git_merge_squash on main from work/<id> — nothing staged.
  4. git_commit(&squash_msg) on main — same has_staged_changes guard fires, NO commit made, returns Ok silently.
  5. Next line reads git_resolve_sha(HEAD) and stuffs it into task.delivered_in. But HEAD is unchanged — so delivered_in gets the *previous* commit's sha. Not this task's.
  6. State branch records review+close normally; main has no [bl-xxxx] tag.
  7. detect_half_push (src/commands/sync.rs:72) later scans for reviewed+closed tasks with no [bl-xxxx] tag on main, finds these, warns. The warning message frames this as a half-push failure ('state landed, main didn't'), which is misleading — the truth is 'review produced no commit'.

The existing carveout in detect_half_push's doc comment already acknowledges the concept: 'Tasks closed via bl update status=closed without ever being reviewed are excluded — they legitimately never produce a main commit.' No-op reviews belong in the same bucket.

=== Fix (Option A, cleanest) ===
In review_worktree:
  - After git_merge_squash, check has_staged_changes on store.root.
  - If empty: skip git_commit on main, and set delivered_in = None on the task. (Do not stamp HEAD's sha.) Log/print a one-liner noting 'no code delivered — checkpoint review'.
  - If non-empty: existing path, git_commit + record delivered_in = new HEAD sha.

In detect_half_push:
  - Also skip tasks whose archived/state JSON has delivered_in = None. Only warn when delivered_in is Some(sha) but the [bl-xxxx] subject isn't reachable — that is an actual half-push.

This means gate tasks and other no-op reviews close cleanly, delivered_in is honest ('nothing was delivered'), and the warning fires only on real half-pushes.

=== Options considered and rejected ===
B. --allow-empty on the squash commit so every review has a [bl-xxxx] commit on main. Preserves 'one commit per review' invariant but noisy — every gate close becomes an empty commit on main, cluttering git log --oneline.
C. Push gates through bl update status=closed instead of bl review. Sidesteps the bug but bl update status=closed currently rejects claimed tasks, and the solo-agent skill flow documents claim->review->close. Would require more code + doc churn than A.

=== Test plan ===
- Unit test: review_worktree with an empty worktree (no changes to deliver) -> task ends in Review state with delivered_in = None; main log has no new commit; no orphan branches or uncommitted state.
- Unit test: normal non-empty review still records delivered_in = sha and produces the [bl-xxxx] commit.
- Unit test for detect_half_push: task with delivered_in = None + state-branch close + no main tag -> NOT warned. Task with delivered_in = Some(sha) + state-branch close + no main tag -> warned (existing behavior preserved).
- Existing review tests still pass.

=== Out of scope ===
- Retroactively rewriting the 11 already-archived lernie task JSONs to null their delivered_in. State-branch history is historical; leaving it alone is fine once the warning is quiet.
- Any changes to the claim->review->close solo flow or gate semantics. Gates stay a close-time blocker on the parent; they just don't need to pretend they produced a commit.

=== Impact ===
Correctness bug: delivered_in can't be trusted for any task where review was a no-op. Tooling that follows delivered_in -> git show will land on the wrong commit.
Noise bug: detect_half_push's warning becomes a false-positive drumbeat in repos that use gates heavily, which is ~every repo that follows the recommended epic+gates pattern.
title: review: empty squash leaves bogus delivered_in; half-push false-positives
